principles.principle: Prefer Roo Code's native file operation tools over MCP FileSystem tools for all workspace file operations. Use read_file, write_to_file, apply_diff, list_files, and search_files for reading, writing, editing, listing, and searching files within the workspace. MCP FileSystem tools (mcp--windows-mcp--FileSystem) are only for accessing files outside the workspace directory or when native tools genuinely cannot accomplish the task.

If Default Shell is cmd.exe, prefer cmd.exe built-ins (del, copy, move, type, mkdir, rmdir) for simple file operations. For complex tasks that cmd.exe cannot handle (byte-level transforms, JSON manipulation, regex over files), invoke PowerShell explicitly via `powershell -NoProfile -Command "..."`. Never write raw PowerShell cmdlets directly — they must be wrapped in a `powershell` invocation.
Do not include runtime implementation details (no function names, command names, UI entry points, or execution syntax)
Prefer the smallest change that satisfies the request
Prefer a single source of truth; avoid duplicated rules across files
Ask a clarifying question only when critical ambiguity remains

boundaries.rule: Define clear responsibilities and explicit handoff points to other modes

validation.rule: After changes, scan for contradictions and update examples to match
